title: Rapper Flavor Flav donates sports equipment to Cambridge youth center
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Rapper Flavor Flav donated sports equipment to Frisoli Youth Center in Cambridge, aiding programs for youth and teens.
The donation included basketballs, dodgeballs, volleyballs, soccer equipment, tennis balls, kickballs, and cornhole gear.
Flavor Flav worked with Harvard College and Franklin Sports on the donation supporting after-school and evening programs.

title: Jaylen Brown has a candid answer when asked if he deserves NBA MVP this season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown is averaging a career-best 28.8 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 5.2 assists this NBA season.
Boston Celtics secured the No. 2 seed in the Eastern Conference with a 54-26 record under Brown's leadership.
Brown is candid about not being a favorite for MVP but emphasizes his contributions and leadership this season.

title: Special World Cup ticket sales shatter Commuter Rail records in first day
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: The MBTA sold over 17,450 special $80 Commuter Rail tickets for World Cup games on the first day of sales.
Nearly 11,000 tickets were sold in the first hour, breaking previous ticket sale records, the MBTA reported.
The World Cup matches will be played at Gillette Stadium, rebranded as Boston Stadium, with 14 trains running each match day.

title: The Boston Fleet are ready to take a sold-out TD Garden by storm
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Fleet will play their first game at a sold-out TD Garden against the Montreal Victoire on Saturday.
Fleet stars Megan Keller, Aerin Frankel, and Haley Winn were celebrated for their Olympic gold wins during a Bruins game.
Boston's success is driven by Aerin Frankel's stellar goalkeeping, with a .955 save percentage and seven shutouts this season.

title: Fall River woman who led sex trafficking ring with 5 others pleads guilty
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Christy Parker, 28, of Fall River, pled guilty to sex trafficking by force, fraud, or coercion.
Parker admitted to trafficking two minors and one adult, using coercion tactics from January to August 2023.
Parker faces up to life in prison, following her involvement with five co-defendants in the trafficking ring.

title: Police look to identify owners of 2 dogs found dead on South Shore
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Weymouth police seek owners of two deceased dogs found in separate locations on the South Shore.
A Terrier-type dog was discovered on Whitmans Pond with no collar or microchip, non-suspicious death.
A Pitbull mix found on Lakeshore Road in a plastic bag, deemed suspicious; public's help requested.

title: She’s running Boston in memory of her son, and for  necrotizing enterocolitis
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jennifer Canvasser, 42, from Davis, California, is running the Boston Marathon in memory of her son, Micah, who died from necrotizing enterocolitis.
Canvasser founded the NEC Society, a leading nonprofit dedicated to the disease, after Micah's death before his first birthday.
She will present on NEC care at the Pediatric Academic Societies Meeting in Boston following the marathon on April 20.

title: Braintree man sentenced to life in prison for murdering Salem State student 40 years ago
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: John Carey, 66, sentenced to life for the 1986 murder of Salem State student Claire Gravel, with no parole eligibility.
Carey was indicted in 2022 after DNA evidence linked him to the crime, found guilty in Essex County Superior Court.
Gravel's family expressed relief at sentencing; they have waited 40 years for justice for their murdered relative.

title: ‘It’s pure excitement’: What James Hagens said after first practice with Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens participated in his first practice with the Boston Bruins at Warrior Ice Arena.
Coach Marco Sturm praised Hagens's adaptability and considers debuting him in upcoming games against the Lightning.
Hagens aims to enhance the Bruins' power play, ranked 27th since February, by leveraging his playmaking skills.

title: Report: 76ers star Joel Embiid to undergo surgery for appendicitis
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Joel Embiid will undergo surgery for appendicitis, missing critical games for the 76ers.
Philadelphia 76ers are in seventh place with five games left in the regular season.
Embiid has played 38 games this season, averaging 26.9 points and 7.7 rebounds.
